data.title: McLane Company’s “100 Days of Summer” Showcases Innovative LTOs Built for Peak-Season C-Store Sales
data.description: In Temple, Texas, McLane Company, Inc., a major American distributor and key partner to top retail and restaurant brands, has introduced the "100 Days of Summer" promotion. This campaign features a range of seasonal food and beverage options tailored to assist convenience retailers in capitalizing on summer sales. With road trips being the prevalent choice for summer travel, making up over 60% of travel plans in the US, McLane's campaign aims to enhance sales during this season.

data.title: The More the Cheesier! GOODLES Launches Wild Wild Pesto Mac & Cheese and Expands Cups Line With Twist My Parm Flavor
data.description: In Santa Cruz, California, GOODLES is introducing a new flavor, Wild Wild Pesto, in their popular Cups line. The long-awaited flavor combines basil-y and garlicky goodness in a creamy sauce coating spiral noodles. Along with this, Twist My Parm is also being added to the Cups line. Wild Wild Pesto aims to satisfy customer requests for a pesto-infused mac & cheese experience. The brand, known for reimagining boxed mac & cheese, is expanding its product offerings with these additions.

data.title: Upland Software to Release First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on May 1, 2026
data.description: Upland Software, Inc. (Nasdaq: UPLD) based in Austin, Texas, will unveil its financial performance for Q1 2026 on May 1, 2026, before the market opens. Specializing in AI-driven knowledge and content management software solutions, Upland Software aims to enable businesses to access essential knowledge, streamline content processes, and achieve tangible returns on investment. Their technologies contribute to elevating customer and employee experiences, all while ensuring compliance with regulations.

data.title: Course of the Annual General Meeting
data.description: On April 28, 2026, Tivoli A/S conducted its Annual General Meeting where the annual report was sanctioned. The Board of Directors suggested a dividend distribution of 26% of the post-tax result, totaling DKK 31.6 million. This equates to DKK 5.5 per share for each shareholding valued at DKK 10.

data.title: Once Upon a Farm Raises the Bar with New Public Benefit Corporation Advisory Board
data.description: Once Upon a Farm, a well-known brand offering organic, farm-fresh nutritious snacks and meals to families, has revealed the establishment of its first Public Benefit Corporation (PBC) Advisory Board. This board consists of industry experts from various backgrounds who will provide strategic direction to the company as it strives to provide top-notch, farm-fresh nutrition and generate meaningful positive change. Once Upon a Farm operates under the ticker symbol OFRM on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: 94% of Contractors Say Site Access Delays Stem From Process Issues, New TEAM Survey Finds
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma, a recent survey conducted by TEAM highlighted the impact of access delays on worksites. The TEAM 2026 Contractor Compliance Bottleneck Survey revealed that 94% of contractors attribute site access delays to process issues rather than the actual requirements. This survey, conducted in March, sheds light on the challenges faced by worksites as they strive to maintain schedules and secure contractor labor. Access delays, even small ones, can lead to significant problems with staffing and project start dates, emphasizing the need for efficiency in addressing these issues.

data.title: OrgChart Releases New Research That Exposes the Most Common Barriers to Successful Workforce Planning
data.description: A recent study by OrgChart, a prominent organizational chart and human resources planning tool, indicates that HR leaders are encountering a multitude of obstacles in workforce planning, potentially jeopardizing their company's long-term strategy and staff retention. The report titled The State of Workforce Planning: 2026, sourced insights from over 400 HR executives based in the United States. While these leaders claim to be proactively strategizing for the future, they admitted to numerous challenges.

data.title: Stablecoin transfer volume decreases by 19% despite increasing supply, holders, and active addresses.
data.description: In the span of one month, the volume of stablecoin transfers decreased by over 19%, while the supply, number of holders, and active addresses saw a consistent increase.
